name: Spinach, Mushroom and Gruyere Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
FILLING:
1 1/2 pounds cream cheese (room temperature)
1/4 cup whipping cream
1/2 teaspoon salt
1/4 teaspoon freshly grated nutmeg
1/4 teaspoon ground red pepper
4 large eggs
4 ounces Gruyere cheese (shredded)
10 ounces frozen chopped spinach (thawed and pressed dry)
2 1/2 tablespoons finely chopped green onion
3 tablespoons unsalted butter
1/2 pound mushrooms (finely chopped)
Salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Melt butter into a 9-inch springform pan. Mix breadcrumbs and melted butter.
3. Press mixture firmly onto the bottom and sides of the pan.
4. Bake until set, about 8 to 10 minutes. Cool.
5. In a blender or food processor, mix cream cheese, whipping cream, salt, nutmeg, and red pepper until smooth. Blend in eggs.
6. Divide filling evenly between two medium bowls. Stir Gruyere into one half.
7. Mix spinach and onion into the remaining mixture. Pour spinach filling into pan.
8. Melt remaining butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add mushrooms and cook until all moisture is evaporated, about 10 minutes, stirring frequently. Season with salt and pepper.
9. Spoon mushrooms over spinach filling. Carefully pour cheese filling over top.
10. Place the pan on the baking sheet. Bake at 325°F for 1 to 1 1/4 hours.
11. Turn off oven and cool cheesecake about 1 hour with the door ajar.
12. Transfer to a rack. Remove springform. Cool to room temperature before serving.
